topic.what: Compute-in-Transit architectural model embedding computation within data movement paths rather than at discrete processing nodes
topic.how: Aligns computation with dataflow using photonic technologies that apply transformations directly to signals in transit, reducing intermediate storage and repeated transfers
topic.why_matters: Data movement has become the dominant constraint in modern workloads (FHE, PQC, AI) where arithmetic intensity falls below 1 op/byte; current optimizations yield diminishing returns when data movement dominates performance and energy cost

characteristics.what_it_measures: Operations per byte in memory-bound workloads
characteristics.evidence: FHE workloads showing memory bandwidth rather than arithmetic cost dominates key switching operations
characteristics.context: Fully homomorphic encryption workloads; evaluation keys reach hundreds of MB

characteristics.what_itasures: Proportion of system cost attributed to data movement vs computation
characteristics.evidence: Memory hierarchy, interconnect, and distributed infrastructure costs increasingly govern performance across FHE, PQC, AI
characteristics.context: Increasingly evident as data volumes grow and computations become more structured

characteristics.evidence: Photonics applies FFT/NTT/polynomial ops directly to propagating optical signals
characteristics.context: Optical interference between coherent light beams performs Fourier-domain transformations as signal propagates

characteristics.evidence: FHE, lattice PQC, AI tensor operations exhibit repeated structured transformations on large data
characteristics.context: Deterministic streaming workloads enable joint scheduling of computation and data movement

novelty: Compute-in-Transit as formal architectural model distinct from near-memory/in-network computing
Photonics enabling computation embedded within signal propagation rather than at discrete nodes
System-level transition from compute-bound to data-movement-bound architectural constraint
Unification of computation and communication as single physical process via optical transformations
open_problems: Practical realization requires execution models expressing computation as structured dataflow
Compiling streaming workloads for photonics-enabled data paths needs new compilation strategies
Hybrid electronic-photonic system architecture optimization remains unsolved
Manufacturing scalability and error correction for photonic compute-in-transit systems
